intro_script: Welcome to your daily dose of bots and bytes! Today, June 16th, we've got eight updates. We’re diving into world-action AI models, the Einride IPO news, and groundbreaking eVTOL certifications from Indonesia. Tune in!
outro_script: And that’s your robotics roundup for June 16th! From world models to real action, what amazing things our bots are learning. Thanks for tuning in, keep exploring the intelligence around us!

items.title: Pretrained to Imagine, Fine-Tuned to Act: The Rise of World-Action Models
items.summary: World-Action Models (WAMs) represent an advancement in robot policy design. These models begin with a pre-trained Vision-Language Model (VLM) backbone for initial capabilities. They are then fine-tuned to translate high-level linguistic instructions into physical actions. Essentially, WAMs bridge the gap between understanding natural language and executing complex physical tasks. This architecture allows robots to move beyond simple recognition toward goal-directed action planning.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "Pretrained to Imagine, Fine-Tuned to Act: The Rise of World-Action Models," a significant advancement in robot policy design has been reported. These World-Action Models aim to bridge the gap between natural language understanding and complex physical execution. The methodology involves utilizing a pre-trained Vision-Language Model as the foundational backbone. This base model is then specifically fine-tuned, enabling it to translate high-level linguistic instructions directly into executable physical actions. This architectural design moves robotic systems beyond mere object recognition toward sophisticated goal-directed action planning. This development marks a notable step in achieving embodied artificial intelligence capabilities across various operational environments.

items.title: Autonomous freight developer Einride goes public via SPAC
items.summary: Einride, an autonomous freight developer based in Stockholm, is publicly listing through a SPAC merger. The company operates a fleet of electric, heavy-duty trucks. Its services provide logistics support to major Global Fortune 500 companies. Operations are currently active across both the United States and Europe. This news was initially reported by The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Autonomous freight developer Einride goes public via SPAC." This story reports that the Swedish company is completing its transition to a publicly traded entity through a merger with a Special Purpose Acquisition Company. Operating an electric heavy-duty truck fleet, Einride currently supplies logistics support to several major Global Fortune 500 organizations across operational bases in both the United States and Europe. The development marks a significant capital infusion into autonomous logistics infrastructure providers, setting a benchmark for future listings within the automated transport sector.

items.title: Indonesia grants world-first overseas certification validation to AutoFlight eVTOL
items.summary: AutoFlight's V2000CG CarryAll has received a Validated Type Certificate (VTC) from Indonesia. This makes it the first eVTOL globally to obtain overseas type certificate validation. The certification confirms the aircraft’s design meets airworthiness standards for two major bodies. Specifically, compliance was verified with both China's CAAC and Indonesia’s DGCA.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Indonesia grants world-first overseas certification validation to AutoFlight eVTOL." This report concerns the achievement of a significant international aviation milestone regarding an electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing aircraft. The company's V2000CG CarryAll has successfully obtained a Validated Type Certificate from Indonesian authorities, marking it as the first such global instance. This certification confirms that the aircraft’s design adheres to airworthiness standards established by two major regulatory bodies. Specifically, verification of compliance was achieved with both China’s CAAC and Indonesia’s DGCA. The validation points toward an increasing standardization process for advanced aerial vehicle technology across national borders.

items.title: Built Robotics, Penn xLAB to develop physical AI for construction
items.summary: Built Robotics and Penn xLAB are collaborating to advance artificial intelligence in the construction industry. This partnership aims to collect more extensive data specifically related to construction sites. The gathered data will be used to improve existing AI models. A key focus of this development is enhancing safety protocols within construction environments. Through this effort, they plan to create physical, functional AI applications for the sector.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Built Robotics, Penn xLAB to develop physical AI for construction." This story concerns a partnership aiming to advance artificial intelligence applications directly within the building and construction sector. Built Robotics and Penn xLAB are collaborating on this initiative with the primary goal of collecting extensive data unique to active construction sites. This gathered information will subsequently be used to refine and enhance established artificial intelligence models, placing a specific emphasis on improving safety protocols prevalent in hazardous construction environments. The outcome of this combined effort is slated to be the creation of tangible, functional AI applications designed for deployment within the industry. Industry analysts anticipate this development marks a significant move toward integrating advanced robotics into manual labor sectors.

items.title: PSYONIC partners with ABB Robotics to apply human touch to robot dexterity
items.summary: PSYONIC is collaborating with ABB Robotics, integrating the company’s Ability Hand with ABB's GoFa collaborative robot arm. This partnership aims to translate data from prosthetic users into advanced robotic grasping capabilities. By combining these technologies, the effort seeks to imbue robots with a more human-like dexterity and touch. The news regarding this collaboration was originally published on The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "PSYONIC partners with ABB Robotics to apply human touch to robot dexterity." This development involves integrating advanced prosthetic data into commercial robotic systems to enhance tactile manipulation. Specifically, the collaboration centers on combining PSYONIC's Ability Hand technology with ABB's GoFa collaborative robot arm. The methodology involves translating complex usage data gathered from individuals using prosthetics into actionable commands for the robotic arm. Through this integration, researchers aim to equip robots with grasping capabilities that emulate human levels of dexterity and sensitive touch interaction within industrial settings. This partnership marks a tangible advancement toward creating more versatile and physically nuanced automation tools across various sectors.

items.title: AI, Robotics and Automated Mining: The Future of Excavators and Mining Machinery Parts
items.summary: The global mining industry is undergoing a major transformation powered by artificial intelligence, robotics, and full automation. Intelligent excavators and autonomous mining trucks are now standard equipment in modern mines. These technological advancements significantly boost both operational safety and overall production efficiency. Consequently, the standards and demands for professional mining machinery parts have also risen considerably.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "AI, Robotics and Automated Mining: The Future of Excavators and Mining Machinery Parts." This report details how artificial intelligence and robotics are fundamentally reshaping global mining operations through full automation. These technological advancements have established intelligent excavators and autonomous mining trucks as standard operational equipment in modern mining sites. The integration of these systems is reported to substantially increase both workplace safety metrics and overall production throughput within the sector. As a direct consequence of this automation, industry standards for professional-grade mining machinery components are experiencing significant increases in demand and complexity requirements. Industry projections indicate that component manufacturers must rapidly align material science innovations with advanced computational control systems to maintain market relevance.

items.title: ROS Industrial: Bringing the ROS Community Together: Meetups in Heilbronn and Karlsruhe
items.summary: Local ROS Meetups were successfully held in Heilbronn (March) and Karlsruhe (May), demonstrating a robust grassroots interest in the ROS 2 ecosystem. The Heilbronn event featured diverse technical presentations on topics like real-time control, motion planning, and LLM-controlled robots. The Karlsruhe meetup covered broad applications from medical robotics to integrating ROS with industrial CNC machines. Both gatherings underscored the continued importance of face-to-face networking for advancing open-source robotics development. These successful events highlight a growing regional appetite for collaborative knowledge exchange within the ROS community.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Industrial: Bringing the ROS Community Together: Meetups in Heilbronn and Karlsruhe." These recent local meetups confirmed a strong grassroots engagement within the ROS 2 ecosystem. The gathering in Heilbronn featured technical presentations covering specialized areas such as real-time control, motion planning algorithms, and integrations involving large language model controlled robotics. Subsequently, the event held in Karlsruhe demonstrated broader industry applications, ranging from medical robotics to linking ROS platforms with traditional industrial CNC machinery. These successful face-to-face interactions underscore the continued value of direct knowledge exchange for advancing open-source robotics development. This sustained regional appetite suggests increasing collaborative growth across the broader automation sector.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Looking for a repository within ROS2 to support
items.summary: A user with experience in C, Python, ROS2, and control theory is seeking a stable repository or project within the ROS2 ecosystem to contribute to. The individual has provided a GitHub link for potential reviewers to assess their suitability for contributions. Recommendations for suitable starting points are requested by the user. The goal is to become actively involved in developing or supporting existing ROS2 projects.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Looking for a repository within ROS2 to support." The discussion centers on an experienced developer seeking an established project within the ROS2 framework into which contributions can be made. This individual possesses expertise spanning C, Python, and control theory, and has provided a GitHub profile for community review to determine appropriate starting points for active involvement in development or support work across various ROS2 projects. Further recommendations are being solicited from community members regarding suitable areas of contribution within the evolving ecosystem. Industry observers can anticipate increased developer engagement as such skilled contributors integrate into the core development efforts surrounding robotics operating systems.

items.title: Bayesian Optimization for Learning Nonlinear MPC in Autonomous Agent Navigation
items.summary: A map-free framework integrating reactive rolling-horizon planning with nonlinear Model Predictive Control (MPC) addresses autonomous navigation in unknown settings. The system uses LiDAR data to create Gaussian occupancy maps, generating collision-free trajectories via A* search tracked by an MPC formulation. Robustness against parameter sensitivity is enhanced using an offline Bayesian optimization scheme based on Tree-structured Parzen Estimators (TPE). A Gaussian Process surrogate aids in analyzing the resulting parameter landscape. Evaluation on a Unitree Go2 quadruped demonstrated that simulation-tuned parameters maintained high performance on hardware, achieving up to 90.0% navigation success.

items.title: Synthetic-to-Real Pipeline for Safe Landing Zone Detection
items.summary: Autonomous Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle (UAV) safe landings in unstructured areas require precise semantic understanding despite limited annotated aerial data. The proposed solution introduces a perception and data generation pipeline tackling the sim-to-real gap. This system uses a procedural synthetic engine to create photorealistic, automatically annotated urban environments through domain randomization. A OneFormer Transformer is fine-tuned on this generated data for global context resolution in segmentation. Finally, a deterministic module employs an Euclidean Distance Transform to identify safe landing zones with guaranteed obstacle clearance, proving effectiveness via UAVid benchmarking and real-world validation.

items.title: Deep Learning-Based Lunar Crater Terrain Relative Navigation
items.summary: Accurate position estimation for autonomous lunar landings requires robust Terrain Relative Navigation (TRN). A proposed algorithm integrates a deep-learning crater detector with an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). The detector analyzes monocular images from orbit, matching observed craters against a global database using Hungarian assignment and outlier removal. These identified measurements refine the EKF, estimating spacecraft pose in the LCLF frame while constraining radial drift with altitude aiding. Simulations show the TRN can recover navigation accuracy to hundreds of meters even after an initial positional error of up to 5 km.

items.title: TacStyle: Personalizing Tactile Robot Policies using Structured Behavior Representations
items.summary: To allow assistive robots to adapt to individual user tastes, the paper proposes using language to reason about preferred behaviors rather than directly generating them. The method learns a structured latent representation that organizes these user preferences based on trajectory differences. When given a preference prompt, a foundation model interprets this latent space to select a specific value corresponding to the desired behavior. Experiments demonstrate that selecting robot behaviors from this structured latent space enables more precise adaptation to user tastes. This approach achieves better results while requiring substantially fewer preference labels than direct language-conditioned policies.

items.title: VANDERER: Map-Free Exploration using Future-Aware and Visual-Curiosity-Guided Diffusion Policy
items.summary: VANDERER is an exploration framework designed for mobile agents to navigate unseen environments without relying on accurate occupancy maps, which are difficult to generate with limited sensors like monocular cameras. It guides pre-trained diffusion policies by incorporating a Visual Curiosity Module (VCM). This VCM utilizes a navigation world model to predict the outcomes of potential actions and quantifies them using a curiosity cost. The resulting cost actively steers the diffusion process toward generating actions that maximize exploration within the environment. Empirical testing across diverse simulated settings showed VANDERER significantly surpassed existing methods, exploring an average of 13.4% more area than NoMaD.

items.title: Multimodal Physiological Assessment of Contact-Rich Physical Human-Robot Interaction Under Varying Environmental Conditions
items.summary: Investigated physiological responses during contact-rich physical human-robot interaction across 18 environmental combinations varying temperature, noise, and light. Multimodal data—including EDA, sEMG, eye-tracking, and subjective ratings—were collected alongside task performance metrics. Results indicated that while objective performance remained constant regardless of environmental stress, autonomic workload (SCL) increased with elevated temperatures. This suggests operators compensate for thermal discomfort by increasing physiological effort to maintain consistent execution. These findings recommend developing control systems that incorporate real-time physiological data to actively reduce operator workload in complex environments.

items.title: Inference-time Policy Steering via Vision and Touch
items.summary: ViTaL is a framework that steers pre-trained generative robot policies during deployment by using both visual and tactile information for action verification. It models multimodal guidance as a bi-level optimization problem: high-level visual sampling selects the overall behavior, while low-level tactile diffusion editing refines the action sequence to meet local contact requirements. To support outcome-based steering, ViTaL learns a visuo-tactile latent world model and utilizes semantically aligned verifiers, including a text-conditioned tactile reward in latent space. Testing across three real-world, contact-rich tasks showed that ViTaL increased overall success by 51% compared to the base policy. The framework notably outperformed unimodal steering approaches by at least 33%.

items.title: LV-Calib: LiDAR-Camera Extrinsic Calibration with Boundary-Response Modeling
items.summary: LV-Calib is a new framework for estimating LiDAR-camera extrinsic parameters and performing LiDAR boundary-response calibration using a printable planar target. The target simultaneously provides indexed visual measurements via fiducials and structural feature points from circular reflectivity boundaries detectable by LiDAR. Instead of assuming ideal contours, the method refines accurate 3D LiDAR features by handling background point cropping and the broadened transition band at discontinuities. This leads to a weighted reprojection-consistent extrinsic optimization using image observations and refined LiDAR feature alignment. Finally, LV-Calib calibrates the LiDAR boundary response by estimating pitch-yaw-range residual statistics from overlapping boundary samples, achieving high accuracy in experiments.
